en_definition: to give special importance or prominence to something in speech or writing
example1: The professor emphasized the importance of proper citation in academic papers.
example1_ru: Профессор подчеркнул важность правильного цитирования в академических работах.
example2: The report emphasizes that climate change requires urgent action.

en_definition: to state the exact meaning of a word or explain what something is
example1: Please define the term 'photosynthesis' before we proceed with the experiment.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, дайте определение термину «фотосинтез», прежде чем мы продолжим эксперимент.
example2: The contract clearly defines the responsibilities of both parties.

en_definition: a thing that is accepted as true without proof or evidence
example1: The study makes several assumptions about student motivation that may not be valid.
example1_ru: Исследование делает несколько предположений о мотивации студентов, которые могут оказаться неверными.
example2: Don't make assumptions; ask questions if you need clarification.

en_definition: a formal statement given as proof, especially in a court or study
example1: The witness gave testimony that supported the original claim.
example1_ru: Свидетель дал показания, подтверждающие первоначальное заявление.
example2: Her testimony helped researchers understand the event more clearly.

en_definition: a way of dealing with something or a method used to solve a problem
example1: Our approach to teaching mathematics focuses on practical applications.
example1_ru: Наш подход к преподаванию математики ориентирован на практическое применение.
example2: The scientific approach requires careful observation and documentation.

en_definition: an abstract idea or a general understanding of something
example1: The concept of gravity was explained clearly in the physics lecture.
example1_ru: Понятие гравитации было ясно объяснено на лекции по физике.
example2: Understanding this key concept is necessary for the next chapter.

en_definition: a basic structure that supports or organizes something
example1: The theoretical framework of the research is based on social psychology.
example1_ru: Теоретическая база исследования строится на социальной психологии.
example2: We need a clear framework for organizing our project timeline.

en_definition: to develop or obtain something from a source or cause
example1: The word 'biology' is derived from Greek roots meaning life and study.
example1_ru: Слово «биология» происходит от греческих корней, означающих «жизнь» и «изучение».
example2: Scientists derive important conclusions from experimental data.

en_definition: to suggest or indicate something without saying it directly
example1: The author implies that economic growth and environmental protection are connected.
example1_ru: Автор подразумевает, что экономический рост и защита окружающей среды взаимосвязаны.
example2: His silence implied disagreement with the proposed solution.

en_definition: despite what has just been said; used to introduce a contrast
example1: The results were mixed; nonetheless, the study proved useful.
example1_ru: Результаты оказались неоднозначными; тем не менее исследование оказалось полезным.
example2: It rained all day; nonetheless, the festival continued as planned.

en_definition: used to introduce another point or piece of information
example1: In addition, the survey revealed a rise in remote work.
example1_ru: Кроме того, опрос выявил рост числа удалённых сотрудников.
example2: She speaks three languages; in addition, she can code well.

en_definition: used to introduce a statement that is the opposite of what came before
example1: Prices rose in the city; conversely, they fell in rural areas.
example1_ru: Цены в городе выросли; в противоположность этому в сельской местности они упали.
example2: He works best under pressure; conversely, his sister prefers calm routines.

en_definition: a proposed explanation that can be tested through research or experiment
example1: The hypothesis states that increased temperature will accelerate chemical reactions.
example1_ru: Гипотеза утверждает, что повышение температуры ускорит химические реакции.
example2: Our hypothesis needs to be tested with careful experimental design.

en_definition: to investigate a subject or problem very thoroughly
example1: Journalists began to probe into the company's financial records.
example1_ru: Журналисты начали тщательно расследовать финансовую отчётность компании.
example2: The committee will probe into the cause of the delay.

en_definition: to judge the value, quality, or condition of something
example1: Experts appraise the painting before it goes to auction.
example1_ru: Эксперты оценивают картину перед тем, как она отправится на аукцион.
example2: Managers appraise employee performance carefully once every single year.

en_definition: standards or principles by which something is judged or measured
example1: The grading criteria are clearly stated in the syllabus for all assignments.
example1_ru: Критерии выставления оценок чётко прописаны в программе курса для всех заданий.
example2: What criteria should we use to select the best candidate?

en_definition: important enough to have a noticeable effect or value
example1: The research found a significant difference between the two treatment groups.
example1_ru: Исследование выявило значительное различие между двумя группами лечения.
example2: This discovery is significant for our understanding of human development.

en_definition: directly connected to or appropriate to the matter being discussed
example1: Please provide only relevant information to support your research argument.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, приводите только относящуюся к делу информацию для подкрепления своего исследовательского аргумента.
example2: This historical context is relevant to understanding modern society.

en_definition: to get or acquire something, often with effort
example1: Students must obtain written permission before conducting human subject research.
example1_ru: Студенты обязаны получить письменное разрешение перед проведением исследований с участием людей.
example2: Where did you obtain your data for this analysis?

en_definition: to identify or describe something exactly and clearly
example1: Investigators struggled to pin down the exact cause of the fire.
example1_ru: Следователи с трудом смогли точно установить причину пожара.
example2: Doctors could not pin down the source of her headaches.

en_definition: to express something clearly in words or writing
example1: The authors clearly state their main argument in the introduction.
example1_ru: Авторы чётко излагают свой главный тезис во введении.
example2: Please state your position on this controversial issue.

en_definition: to be a sign or symbol of; to indicate something
example1: In mathematics, the symbol X denotes an unknown variable.
example1_ru: В математике символ X обозначает неизвестную переменную.
example2: The red mark denotes areas that require further investigation.

en_definition: a restriction or limitation that prevents something from happening
example1: Budget constraints limited the scope of our research project.
example1_ru: Бюджетные ограничения сузили масштаб нашего исследовательского проекта.
example2: Time constraints prevented us from testing all possible solutions.

en_definition: existing or happening before something else in time or order
example1: Prior research suggests that sleep is essential for memory formation.
example1_ru: Более ранние исследования показывают, что сон необходим для формирования памяти.
example2: You must have prior approval before beginning the experiment.

en_definition: happening after something else in time or as a result of it
example1: Subsequent analysis revealed problems with the initial methodology.
example1_ru: Последующий анализ выявил проблемы в исходной методологии.
example2: The subsequent chapters build on the concepts introduced earlier.

en_definition: existing as a natural, permanent, or inseparable quality
example1: There is an inherent risk in all experimental research procedures.
example1_ru: Всем экспериментальным исследовательским процедурам присущ определённый риск.
example2: The inherent complexity of the system makes prediction difficult.

en_definition: to be the parts that form something; to make up the whole
example1: These three elements constitute the main framework of the theory.
example1_ru: Эти три элемента составляют основную структуру теории.
example2: Student fees constitute a major source of university income.

en_definition: to arrange things so that they match or work together properly
example1: The team worked to align its goals with company strategy.
example1_ru: Команда работала над тем, чтобы согласовать свои цели со стратегией компании.
example2: Managers must align schedules carefully across every single department.

en_definition: to say that something is caused by or belongs to someone or something
example1: Scientists attribute climate change primarily to human greenhouse gas emissions.
example1_ru: Учёные объясняют изменение климата главным образом антропогенными выбросами парниковых газов.
example2: Ancient scholars attributed diseases to imbalances of bodily fluids.

en_definition: to provide evidence or facts that support a claim or statement
example1: Can you substantiate your claim with empirical data from the study?
example1_ru: Можете ли вы подтвердить своё утверждение эмпирическими данными исследования?
example2: The witnesses were unable to substantiate the defendant's alibi.

en_definition: to make something easier or help it happen
example1: Technology facilitates communication between students and teachers.
example1_ru: Технологии облегчают общение между студентами и преподавателями.
example2: Good organization facilitates efficient learning and productivity.

en_definition: to show with evidence that a theory or claim was correct all along
example1: The lawyer could not vindicate a theory with solid proof.
example1_ru: Адвокат не смог подтвердить теорию убедительными доказательствами.
example2: New data helped vindicate a theory proposed decades earlier.

en_definition: to be in opposition to something; to claim the opposite of what was said
example1: The new findings contradict previous assumptions about the disorder.
example1_ru: Новые данные противоречат прежним представлениям об этом расстройстве.
example2: His actions contradicted his stated principles.

en_definition: to suggest an idea or theory for others to consider
example1: Scientists put forth a new theory about the planet's water.
example1_ru: Учёные выдвинули новую теорию о воде на этой планете.
example2: The economist put forth a fresh model to explain rising prices.

en_definition: to give support to a statement or theory by providing more evidence
example1: Two independent studies corroborate the original findings of the report.
example1_ru: Два независимых исследования подтверждают первоначальные выводы отчёта.
example2: Witnesses corroborate the driver's account of the accident.

en_definition: to show or describe something clearly in words or images
example1: The mural depicts the history of the small fishing town.
example1_ru: Фреска изображает историю небольшого рыбацкого городка.
example2: Her novel depicts life in a rural village decades ago.

en_definition: to include a wide range of things within a single category
example1: The course will encompass both theory and practical exercises.
example1_ru: Курс охватит как теорию, так и практические занятия.
example2: Her research interests encompass psychology, biology, and ethics.

en_definition: to remove or get rid of something completely
example1: This filter eliminates confounding variables from the analysis.
example1_ru: Этот фильтр исключает из анализа мешающие переменные.
example2: We can eliminate poor solutions and focus on promising approaches.

en_definition: to make something greater in size, value, or amount
example1: The company plans to augment its staff before the holidays.
example1_ru: Компания планирует увеличить штат перед праздниками.
example2: New evidence can augment our understanding of ancient trade routes.

en_definition: to weaken or damage something; to accept less than desired to reach agreement
example1: Using poor methodology compromises the validity of the findings.
example1_ru: Использование слабой методологии ставит под угрозу достоверность результатов.
example2: They compromise on the deadline to satisfy both parties.

example1: Coastal towns undergo change as tourism increases each year.
example1_ru: Прибрежные города претерпевают изменения по мере роста туризма из года в год.
example2: Languages undergo change slowly as new words enter daily use.
en_definition: to experience a process of gradual transformation over time
to increase in size, range, or scope; to provide more detail
example1: The author expands on this concept in the following chapter.
example1_ru: Автор подробно раскрывает эту концепцию в следующей главе.
example2: We plan to expand our research to include more participants.

en_definition: to gather information slowly and in small amounts from various sources
example1: Researchers glean useful details from old newspaper archives.
example1_ru: Исследователи по крупицам собирают полезные сведения из старых газетных архивов.
example2: She managed to glean a few facts from the brief interview.

en_definition: to notice or become aware of something through the senses or mind
example1: Students perceive this course as more difficult than others.
example1_ru: Студенты воспринимают этот курс как более сложный, чем остальные.
example2: How do different cultures perceive time differently?

en_definition: to succeed in understanding something that is unclear or written in code
example1: Historians worked for years to decipher the ancient script.
example1_ru: Историки годами пытались расшифровать древний текст.
example2: It took a while to decipher the doctor's messy handwriting.

en_definition: to express or measure something as a specific amount or figure
example1: It is difficult to put a number on the exact impact of the policy.
example1_ru: Точно оценить в цифрах реальное влияние этой политики непросто.
example2: The study tries to put a number on customer satisfaction levels.

en_definition: to place two things side by side in order to compare them
example1: The exhibit juxtapose old photographs with modern city scenes.
example1_ru: Выставка сопоставляет старые фотографии с современными городскими пейзажами.
example2: The essay juxtapose two opposing theories about human behavior.

en_definition: to recognize or show the difference between two or more things
example1: Students must differentiate between fact and opinion in the article.
example1_ru: Студенты должны уметь различать факты и мнения в статье.
example2: The test helps doctors differentiate between similar illnesses.

en_definition: to combine separate parts into a unified whole
example1: The new system integrates data from multiple sources effectively.
example1_ru: Новая система эффективно объединяет данные из нескольких источников.
example2: We integrated feedback from students into the course design.

en_definition: to change something slightly to improve it or make it different
example1: We modified the experimental protocol to address safety concerns.
example1_ru: Мы изменили протокол эксперимента, чтобы устранить проблемы с безопасностью.
example2: The original design was modified to reduce production costs.

en_definition: to add something to complete or improve something else
example1: The lecture notes supplement the information in the textbook.
example1_ru: Конспект лекции дополняет информацию из учебника.
example2: We supplement our findings with additional case studies.

en_definition: to strengthen or support something, especially by adding or repeating
example1: Practice reinforces learning and retention of new concepts.
example1_ru: Практика укрепляет усвоение и запоминание новых понятий.
example2: The data reinforces our initial conclusions about the effect.

en_definition: of considerable importance or value; dealing with matters of real concern
example1: The committee made substantive changes to the proposal.
example1_ru: Комитет внёс существенные изменения в предложение.
example2: This research contributes substantive knowledge to the field.

en_definition: extremely thorough, careful, or severe in approach or application
example1: The study followed rigorous scientific methodology throughout.
example1_ru: Исследование строго следовало научной методологии на всех этапах.
example2: Rigorous peer review ensures publication quality in academic journals.

en_definition: strong, firm, and not easily broken or damaged; reliable and effective
example1: The experimental design is robust enough to withstand criticism.
example1_ru: Экспериментальный план достаточно надёжен, чтобы выдержать критику.
example2: Our conclusions are based on robust evidence from multiple studies.

en_definition: logical and consistent; easy to understand and follow
example1: The author presents a coherent argument throughout the dissertation.
example1_ru: Автор выстраивает связную аргументацию на протяжении всей диссертации.
example2: A coherent theory must explain all relevant observations.

en_definition: having more than one possible meaning or interpretation
example1: The wording of the question is ambiguous and needs clarification.
example1_ru: Формулировка вопроса двусмысленна и требует уточнения.
example2: Ambiguous results make it difficult to reach firm conclusions.

en_definition: stated clearly and directly, leaving no doubt about the meaning
example1: The syllabus provides explicit instructions for completing the assignment.
example1_ru: Программа курса даёт четкие инструкции по выполнению задания.
example2: The author makes explicit the connection between theory and practice.

en_definition: suggested or understood but not directly expressed in words
example1: The implicit message of the passage concerns social inequality.
example1_ru: Скрытый смысл отрывка касается социального неравенства.
example2: There is an implicit assumption that all students have background knowledge.

en_definition: coming before or introductory to something more important
example1: Preliminary results suggest a promising direction for further research.
example1_ru: Предварительные результаты указывают на перспективное направление для дальнейших исследований.
example2: A preliminary examination is required before entering the program.

en_definition: based on random choice rather than logical reasoning or system
example1: The selection of participants was not arbitrary but carefully planned.
example1_ru: Отбор участников не был случайным, а был тщательно спланирован.
example2: Arbitrary decisions without data support undermine scientific credibility.

en_definition: clear, obvious, or real enough to be touched or perceived
example1: The research produced tangible results that improved practice.
example1_ru: Исследование принесло конкретные результаты, улучшившие практику.
example2: Tangible evidence supports the theory more strongly than speculation.

en_definition: existing only as an idea or theory, not having physical or practical form
example1: Mathematics deals with abstract concepts and logical relationships.
example1_ru: Математика имеет дело с абстрактными понятиями и логическими взаимосвязями.
example2: Abstract thinking is important for advanced problem solving.

en_definition: dealing with things in a practical, realistic way based on actual circumstances
example1: We took a pragmatic approach to resource allocation.
example1_ru: Мы применили прагматичный подход к распределению ресурсов.
example2: A pragmatic solution is often better than a perfect theoretical one.

en_definition: a structured set of ideas used to organize and explain a topic
example1: The professor introduced a new framework model for the seminar.
example1_ru: Профессор представил на семинаре новую концептуальную схему.
example2: Researchers built a framework model to compare the two economies.

en_definition: a system of principles and practices for conducting research or studies
example1: The methodology used in this study is clearly described in the paper.
example1_ru: Методология, использованная в этом исследовании, чётко описана в статье.
example2: Different methodologies yield different results in some cases.

en_definition: a limit or set of standards that affects how something can be done
example1: The study examined several parameters of student achievement.
example1_ru: В исследовании рассматривалось несколько параметров успеваемости учащихся.
example2: We set specific parameters for the experiment beforehand.

en_definition: a thing that can change or vary; an element that can be altered
example1: Temperature was the independent variable in our experiment.
example1_ru: Температура была независимой переменной в нашем эксперименте.
example2: We controlled all variables except the one being tested.

en_definition: a relationship or connection between two things where they affect each other
example1: There is a strong correlation between study time and exam performance.
example1_ru: Существует сильная корреляция между временем занятий и результатами экзамена.
example2: Correlation does not necessarily imply causation.

en_definition: the relationship between cause and effect; one thing causing another
example1: Proving causation requires more rigorous evidence than showing correlation.
example1_ru: Доказать причинно-следственную связь требует более строгих доказательств, чем показать корреляцию.
example2: The causation chain is complex in this biological system.

en_definition: the amount by which something varies or differs from an average
example1: The variance in test scores indicates different levels of understanding.
example1_ru: Разброс результатов тестов указывает на разный уровень понимания материала.
example2: Statistical variance helps determine the reliability of findings.

en_definition: something that differs from what is normal, expected, or typical
example1: The anomaly in the data could indicate an experimental error.
example1_ru: Аномалия в данных может указывать на ошибку эксперимента.
example2: This geological anomaly puzzles scientists and requires explanation.

en_definition: an earlier event or action that serves as an example for similar situations
example1: There is no precedent for this type of discovery in our field.
example1_ru: Для такого рода открытия в нашей области прецедентов не существует.
example2: The ruling set an important precedent for future cases.

en_definition: a situation or statement that seems to contradict itself yet may be true
example1: This historical paradox challenges our traditional understanding.
example1_ru: Этот исторический парадокс бросает вызов нашему традиционному пониманию.
example2: The paradox highlights the complexity of human behavior.

en_definition: a fact or proposition that provides the basis for an argument
example1: The premise of the study assumes that all students have internet access.
example1_ru: Предпосылка исследования исходит из того, что у всех студентов есть доступ в интернет.
example2: If we accept this premise, then the conclusion follows logically.

en_definition: the quality of being logically sound or well-founded
example1: The validity of the research depends on proper methodology.
example1_ru: Обоснованность исследования зависит от правильной методологии.
example2: Researchers questioned the validity of the original findings.

en_definition: the quality of being dependable and producing consistent results
example1: Test reliability ensures consistent measurement over repeated applications.
example1_ru: Надёжность теста обеспечивает согласованность измерений при повторных применениях.
example2: The reliability of this instrument has been well established.

en_definition: a restriction or weakness that prevents something from being perfect or complete
example1: The study acknowledges several important limitations in its design.
example1_ru: В исследовании признаётся ряд существенных ограничений его дизайна.
example2: Sample size limitation affected the generalizability of findings.

en_definition: a consequence of an action, often complicated or unexpected
example1: The new law could have serious ramification for small businesses.
example1_ru: Новый закон может иметь серьёзные последствия для малого бизнеса.
example2: Nobody fully understood the ramification of the decision at first.

en_definition: the path followed by a moving object or the general direction of development
example1: The trajectory of economic growth shows interesting regional differences.
example1_ru: Траектория экономического роста демонстрирует любопытные региональные различия.
example2: Students' academic trajectory improves with proper support.

en_definition: a level or point at which something changes or begins to take effect
example1: The pain threshold varies significantly among individuals.
example1_ru: Болевой порог значительно варьируется у разных людей.
example2: Unemployment crossed the critical threshold of five percent.

en_definition: a fact or event in nature or society; something notable or remarkable
example1: Climate change is a complex phenomenon affecting multiple systems.
example1_ru: Изменение климата — сложное явление, затрагивающее множество систем.
example2: This psychological phenomenon has fascinated researchers for decades.

en_definition: the size or extent of something; the degree of importance
example1: The magnitude of the effect was greater than we predicted.
example1_ru: Величина эффекта оказалась больше, чем мы предполагали.
example2: Scientists measure earthquake magnitude on the Richter scale.

en_definition: the circumstances or situation that helps explain something
example1: Understanding historical context is essential for interpreting the text.
example1_ru: Понимание исторического контекста необходимо для интерпретации текста.
example2: These results make sense only within the appropriate context.

en_definition: a range or sequence of different types or shades of something
example1: The spectrum of services ranges from basic to advanced support.
example1_ru: Спектр услуг варьируется от базовой до продвинутой поддержки.
example2: Students have a wide spectrum of abilities in this class.

en_definition: a collection of separate things gathered together into one total
example1: The aggregate of all departments shows improved performance.
example1_ru: Совокупные показатели всех отделов демонстрируют улучшение результатов.
example2: Aggregate data masks important variations between groups.

en_definition: a part or share of something; the correct relationship between parts
example1: A large proportion of students prefer online learning options.
example1_ru: Значительная доля студентов предпочитает онлайн-обучение.
example2: The proportion of resources allocated to research increased.

en_definition: a particular part or feature of something
example1: We examined every aspect of the educational system carefully.
example1_ru: Мы внимательно изучили каждый аспект системы образования.
example2: The ethical aspect of the research is particularly important.

en_definition: a measurable extent of something; an aspect or facet of a problem
example1: The problem has economic, social, and political dimensions.
example1_ru: Проблема имеет экономическое, социальное и политическое измерения.
example2: We need to consider the emotional dimension of this issue.

en_definition: the way something works or how it operates; a process or system
example1: The mechanism of photosynthesis involves complex chemical reactions.
example1_ru: Механизм фотосинтеза включает сложные химические реакции.
example2: We need to understand the mechanism behind student motivation.

en_definition: the fundamental reason or logical basis for something
example1: The rationale for this policy change is clearly explained in the report.
example1_ru: Обоснование этого изменения политики чётко изложено в отчёте.
example2: What is the rationale behind your conclusion?

en_definition: a subtle difference or shade of meaning; a fine distinction
example1: The nuance of this argument was missed by early critics.
example1_ru: Нюанс этого аргумента ускользнул от ранних критиков.
example2: Good translation requires understanding cultural nuances.

en_definition: a division into two distinct and usually opposite parts or groups
example1: The dichotomy between theory and practice challenges many students.
example1_ru: Дихотомия между теорией и практикой создаёт трудности для многих студентов.
example2: This dichotomy simplifies a complex reality too much.

en_definition: a change in the direction or force behind a process or movement
example1: The election saw a sudden momentum shift in the final week.
example1_ru: На выборах в последнюю неделю произошёл резкий перелом хода событий.
example2: Analysts noted a momentum shift toward renewable energy sources.

en_definition: to demand or specify something as a requirement or condition
example1: The contract stipulates that payment must be made within thirty days.
example1_ru: Контракт оговаривает, что оплата должна быть произведена в течение тридцати дней.
example2: The agreement stipulates specific responsibilities for each party.

en_definition: to prevent or make something impossible
example1: Confidentiality agreements preclude discussing certain details publicly.
example1_ru: Соглашения о конфиденциальности исключают возможность публичного обсуждения определённых деталей.
example2: Budget limitations preclude hiring additional staff this year.

en_definition: to strongly support or recommend something
example1: The author advocates for greater investment in science education.
example1_ru: Автор отстаивает необходимость увеличения инвестиций в научное образование.
example2: Many educators advocate using technology in the classroom.

en_definition: to argue or insist that something is true
example1: Some researchers contend that these results are not reproducible.
example1_ru: Некоторые исследователи утверждают, что эти результаты невоспроизводимы.
example2: The defendant contends that the evidence was improperly obtained.

en_definition: to express something clearly and effectively in words
example1: The speaker articulated the main points very clearly.
example1_ru: Докладчик очень чётко сформулировал основные положения.
example2: It is difficult to articulate abstract concepts to beginners.

en_definition: to correct something that is wrong or make it right
example1: We need to rectify the errors in the original data immediately.
example1_ru: Нам нужно немедленно исправить ошибки в исходных данных.
example2: Management took steps to rectify the unfair treatment.
